---
title: "get_global_ranking"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `get_global_ranking()`

Global Ranking for a Title Across Countries

## Usage

```r
get_global_ranking(
  title,
  platform_name = "netflix",
  date,
  countries = c("United States", "United Kingdom", "Canada", "Australia", "Germany",
    "France", "Brazil", "India", "Japan", "South Korea", "Mexico", "Spain", "Italy",
    "Netherlands", "Sweden"),
  flix_type = "movies",
  token = "FLIX_PATROL"
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `title` | Character. The title to look up. |
| `platform_name` | Character. Platform name (default '"netflix"'). |
| `date` | Date/Character. The chart date. |
| `countries` | Character vector. Country names to check. Defaults to a broad set of 15 major markets. |
| `flix_type` | Character. Content type (default '"movies"'). |
| `token` | Character. API environment variable name. |

## Value

A tibble with columns 'country', 'rank', 'title', 'date'.
Countries where the title is not in the Top 10 return 'rank = NA'.

## Description

For a given title and date, fetches its Top 10 rank in every country provided,
showing geographic reach and relative performance.
